tasks.title: make bootstrap_three_docs.py --refresh preserve closer-set task status
tasks.target: make bootstrap_three_docs.py --refresh preserve closer-set task status
acceptance: bootstrap_three_docs.py --refresh on a registry where a task has status=done + last_outcome=committed leaves those two fields unchanged. A new pytest under .claude/bin/tests/ enforces this for all closer-final statuses. Manual patch of registry.json after refresh is no longer needed.
verification_commands: Set task P01-S02-T001.status=done in registry.json. Run python3 -B -S .claude/bin/bootstrap_three_docs.py --refresh. Assert: (a) refresh exits 0
(b) git diff registry.json shows the task's status

triage.why_not_debugger: Bug lives in the orchestrator framework (.claude/bin/bootstrap_three_docs.py + supporting modules), not in any app slice Write set. The bug only manifests when bootstrap_three_docs.py --refresh runs on a registry that has tasks at status=done from recent closers — refresh regenerates task records and resets the status to ready_for_close. I patched the registry manually for T002 (commit 570b702) but the root cause persists for any future refresh. Debugger inside T002 cannot fix orchestrator framework code without expanding Write set across the entire .claude/ static config.

notes: Description: bootstrap_three_docs.py --refresh, when re-run after a slice closer has committed, overwrites the 'done' status (and last_outcome/last_updated_by/last_stop_at) back to the registry-template defaults (ready_for_close / ready). The refresh message says 'Runtime state preserved by default (62 matching task(s))' but the diff shows fields like status, last_outcome, last_updated_by being reset. Manifested for P01-S02-T002 when STACK_PROFILE.yaml was changed and bootstrap --refresh was run for propagation: T002 status flipped from 'done' to 'ready_for_close' despite the closer's atomic commit being merged to main. Fix: in the registry merger, when a task already exists with status in {done, blocked, skipped} and last_outcome in {committed, deployed}, do NOT overwrite those lifecycle fields — only update title/depends_on/conflict_groups/write_set/journey_refs/acceptance derived from the Coverage Registry. Add a regression test under .claude/bin/tests/ that creates a registry with status=done and asserts --refresh preserves it.

Triage: out_of_scope; why_not_debugger=Bug lives in the orchestrator framework (.claude/bin/bootstrap_three_docs.py + supporting modules), not in any app slice Write set. The bug only manifests when bootstrap_three_docs.py --refresh runs on a registry that has tasks at status=done from recent closers — refresh regenerates task records and resets the status to ready_for_close. I patched the registry manually for T002 (commit 570b702) but the root cause persists for any future refresh. Debugger inside T002 cannot fix orchestrator framework code without expanding Write set across the entire .claude/ static config.

tasks.title: generate ≥32-byte JWT dev key + default ENABLE_VERBOSE_LOGGING in dev .env
tasks.target: generate ≥32-byte JWT dev key + default ENABLE_VERBOSE_LOGGING in dev .env
acceptance: After a fresh setup-from-scratch.sh run on a clean repo
.env contains JWT_PRIVATE_KEY with len>=32 (real random
not 'replace-with-dev-key') AND ENABLE_VERBOSE_LOGGING=true. Backend startup no longer warns about JWT key length. .env.example documents both.
verification_commands: assert value length >= 32 and is not 'replace-with-dev-key'
assert no 'JWT_PRIVATE_KEY too short' warning in startup logs.

triage.why_not_debugger: The .env file in the repo (not .env.example) is developer-local state, outside the T002 Write set. The placeholder 'replace-with-dev-key' was set by setup-from-scratch and never rotated; tokens.py correctly emits a warning at startup but cannot fail-hard without breaking other devs. Fix belongs to a dedicated config-hygiene slice that generates a real ≥32-byte random dev key, wires ENABLE_VERBOSE_LOGGING=true as the dev default, and updates setup-from-scratch.sh to provision both. None of this fits the sign-in canonical Write set.

notes: Description: Two hygiene issues surfaced during P01-S02-T002 /verify-slice: (1) JWT_PRIVATE_KEY in .env is 'replace-with-dev-key' (20 bytes < 32, RFC 7518 §3.2 violation for HS256). tokens.py:62-73 emits a startup warning but does not fail. (2) ENABLE_VERBOSE_LOGGING is not declared in .env at all, so dev-restart.sh starts the backend with WARNING-level logging by default — verify-slice has to override it manually. Fix: extend scripts/setup-from-scratch.sh (or add a new scripts/gen-dev-secrets.sh) to (a) generate a 64-char random JWT_PRIVATE_KEY via 'python3 -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_urlsafe(48))"' if the current value matches the placeholder pattern, (b) set ENABLE_VERBOSE_LOGGING=true in .env if missing, (c) document both behaviors in .env.example. Acceptance: clean clone + setup yields .env where JWT_PRIVATE_KEY length >= 32 and ENABLE_VERBOSE_LOGGING == 'true'.

Triage: out_of_scope; why_not_debugger=The .env file in the repo (not .env.example) is developer-local state, outside the T002 Write set. The placeholder 'replace-with-dev-key' was set by setup-from-scratch and never rotated; tokens.py correctly emits a warning at startup but cannot fail-hard without breaking other devs. Fix belongs to a dedicated config-hygiene slice that generates a real ≥32-byte random dev key, wires ENABLE_VERBOSE_LOGGING=true as the dev default, and updates setup-from-scratch.sh to provision both. None of this fits the sign-in canonical Write set.

tasks.title: fix dev-restart.profile.sh verification-data bootstrap source path
tasks.target: fix dev-restart.profile.sh verification-data bootstrap source path
acceptance: scripts/dev-restart.sh --reset completes the seed step (loads data/verification users) without falling back to the manual workaround. tests/orchestrator-level smoke covers the seed assertion (count > 0).
verification_commands: Run scripts/dev-restart.sh --reset on a clean DB. Assert: (a) script exits 0

triage.why_not_debugger: Bug is in scripts/dev-restart.profile.sh, not in the P01-S02-T002 sign-in write set (backend/app/auth/**). Debugger inside T002 cannot touch dev tooling without expanding Write set / Conflict group, which is exactly what FU exists for. The bug exists since the profile was first added and was just unmasked because T002 was the first slice to use --reset after the verification_data loader fix (P00-S02-T004) landed; not a regression introduced by sign-in.

notes: Description: scripts/dev-restart.profile.sh:351-352 invokes 'python -m app.verification_data.bootstrap --source data/verification' from cwd=$HILO_BACKEND_DIR (=backend/), but data/verification lives at repo root. Result: db_reset() always fails the seed step with 'verification data directory not found' and silently warns instead of seeding. /verify-slice --reset works around it manually but the hard reset is no longer self-contained. Fix: cd to ROOT_DIR before invoking the bootstrap, or pass --source "${ROOT_DIR}/data/verification" explicitly. Verify by running scripts/dev-restart.sh --reset on a clean DB and asserting the seed step reports inserted>=1 with no warning.

Triage: out_of_scope; why_not_debugger=Bug is in scripts/dev-restart.profile.sh, not in the P01-S02-T002 sign-in write set (backend/app/auth/**). Debugger inside T002 cannot touch dev tooling without expanding Write set / Conflict group, which is exactly what FU exists for. The bug exists since the profile was first added and was just unmasked because T002 was the first slice to use --reset after the verification_data loader fix (P00-S02-T004) landed; not a regression introduced by sign-in.
